spec_match.rationale: The explorer no longer uses customElements.get() or nonexistent content attributes; it probes HTMLUserMediaElement and generates setConstraints() plus stream handling code.

interactive.rationale: Users can toggle requested video/audio/facing constraints, see generated native and fallback code, and activate the native element or fallback button.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now describes configuration of the capture request and fallback content, matching the current proposal.

edge_cases.rationale: The page handles unsupported native elements, no-track selections, native error/cancel events, and fallback getUserMedia errors.

references.rationale: The page sits under the correct feature and links back to the feature context.

design_system.rationale: The page uses contrast-safe variables, stable controls, generated code output, and a real live preview.

summary: The explorer now teaches the current <usermedia> interface: HTMLUserMediaElement detection, setConstraints(), stream/error/cancel events, and fallback content.
